sources.author: Swyx
sources.headline: AI Researcher & Developer Advocate
sources.post_summary: Discusses the evolution of AI agents from hype to practical deployment, highlighting key frameworks like LangChain and AutoGPT for building autonomous systems. Shares insights on challenges in reliability and scaling agentic workflows. Emphasizes the need for better developer tools to streamline agent orchestration.

sources.author: Andrej Karpathy
sources.headline: Founder, Eureka Labs; Former Director of AI at Tesla
sources.post_summary: Explores the infrastructure required for training and deploying AI agents at scale, comparing GPU clusters vs. TPUs. Details optimizations in data pipelines and model serving for real-time agent interactions. Predicts a shift towards agent-native infrastructure in the next year.

sources.author: Guillermo Rauch
sources.headline: CEO, Vercel
sources.post_summary: Announces new developer tools for AI agent deployment on edge networks, focusing on low-latency automation. Demonstrates how Vercel's platform reduces setup time for agentic apps from days to minutes. Includes code snippets for integrating AI agents with Next.js.

sources.author: Nat Friedman
sources.headline: Co-Founder, GitHub (former)
sources.post_summary: Analyzes the rise of open-source AI infrastructure projects like Ray and MLflow for agent orchestration. Shares metrics on adoption rates and case studies from enterprises automating dev workflows. Calls for more collaboration between devs and AI researchers.

sources.author: Addy Osmani
sources.headline: Engineering Leader, Google Chrome
sources.post_summary: Dives into browser-based AI agents and tools for web automation, leveraging WebGPU for local inference. Outlines best practices for building performant developer tools that run agents client-side. Highlights privacy benefits and future Chrome integrations.

sources.author: Sami Rahman
sources.headline: Founder @ RevGenius | GTM Engineering Expert
sources.post_summary: Discusses building scalable GTM engines by integrating engineering with sales processes. Shares a framework for automating outbound at scale while maintaining personalization. Emphasizes data-driven iteration for revenue growth.

sources.author: Dan Disney
sources.headline: CEO @ Sales Geek | Sales Leadership Coach
sources.post_summary: Explores how sales ops teams can leverage outbound automation tools to boost pipeline without burning out reps. Provides case study on 3x-ing leads through AI-driven sequences. Stresses alignment with revenue ops for long-term success.

sources.author: Lara Hogan
sources.headline: VP of Revenue Operations @ Scale.com
sources.post_summary: Outlines a content strategy playbook for RevOps teams to fuel GTM motions. Details how to repurpose sales enablement content into outbound assets. Highlights metrics showing 40% uplift in response rates.

sources.author: Kyle Coleman
sources.headline: Head of Sales Ops @ Gong.io
sources.post_summary: Breaks down RevOps transformation using automation in sales ops workflows. Shares lessons from implementing outbound systems that integrate with CRM for real-time optimization. Focuses on cross-functional collaboration for GTM efficiency.

sources.author: April Dunford
sources.headline: Positioning Evangelist | Author of Obviously Awesome
sources.post_summary: Connects content strategy to GTM positioning for sales teams. Advises on creating outbound content that resonates with buyer psychology. Includes examples from B2B SaaS revenue ops playbooks.

sources.author: Pieter Levels
sources.headline: Indie Maker & Founder of Nomad List, Remote OK
sources.post_summary: Pieter shared his journey as a solo developer shipping multiple products like Nomad List and Remote OK without VC funding. He emphasized bootstrapping, iterating based on user feedback, and building in public to gain traction. The post details his revenue milestones and tips for other solo founders.

sources.author: Arvid Kahl
sources.headline: Serial Entrepreneur, Author of The Bootstrapped Founder
sources.post_summary: Arvid recounted pivoting from corporate job to bootstrapped SaaS founder, shipping his first product FeedbackPanda. He discussed the challenges of solo building, validating ideas publicly, and scaling to $300k ARR. Key advice includes focusing on niche problems and consistent shipping.

sources.author: KP (Karthik Pattabiraman)
sources.headline: Solo Founder @ Shipfa.st
sources.post_summary: KP documented his 30-day challenge building and shipping Shipfa.st as a solo dev, from idea to launch. He shared daily updates on tech stack, user acquisition via building in public, and early revenue. The post inspires others to ship fast without teams.

sources.author: Sahil Lavingia
sources.headline: Founder & CEO @ Gumroad
sources.post_summary: Sahil shared his career pivot from Pinterest to founding Gumroad as a solo builder initially. He highlighted lessons from shipping MVPs publicly, adapting to market feedback, and sustaining as a one-person business. Post includes revenue transparency and founder mental health tips.

sources.author: Daniel Vassallo
sources.headline: Ex-AWS, Indie Maker @ OnePersonBusiness
sources.post_summary: Daniel detailed his pivot from Amazon engineer to solo founder, shipping small products like PDF-to-Notion tools. He advocates building in public for accountability and shares how side projects led to full-time independence with multiple launches.
